name: Mobile Phase 1 — Authority Spine
outcome_goal: The UI-independent authority stack lands: WebAuthn register/assert/step-up ceremonies, mobile session middleware, plan-envelope stamp at submission, capability resolver, and the three oversight runtime modules (blast-radius tracker, policy-drift detector, autonomy-chain ledger) all pass integration tests against the existing migrations 185-189. No mobile surface yet; this phase establishes the authority spine that Phase 2 rewires the command bus onto.
anti_requirements: Do NOT ship any PWA code. The mobile surface lands in Phase 5.
Do NOT touch _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES in control_commands.py — the rewire is Phase 2.
Do NOT introduce Redis or any cache between classify_control_intent and Praxis.db.

Do NOT widen writes outside Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/, Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/webauthn/, Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/, Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/, and Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/.
Do NOT roll custom ed25519 HTTP signatures. Auth is WebAuthn Passkey (FIDO2 L3) via py_webauthn; bootstrap uses a single-use enrollment token only.
notes: Lifted verbatim from config/cascade/specs/W_mobile_access_plan_20260420.queue.json waves 0-2. Migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied; oversight tables (policy_drift_events, autonomy_chain_ledger, session_blast_radius, session_blast_radius_policy) exist. Migrations 185-188 are scheduled as wave0_migration_capability_ledger + wave1_session_middleware within this phase.

jobs.label: wave0_integrated_design
jobs.description: Produce the integrated design doc that downstream waves consume.
jobs.outcome_goal: A committed design at docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md naming every new/modified table, endpoint, Python module, TypeScript module, and the end-to-end call graph from chat_orchestrator.request_workflow_submit_command through plan-envelope stamp, resolver, classify_control_intent, and gate_evaluations insert.
jobs.prompt:
Write docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md.

Read first:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py (lines 423-470, 822-860 choke point)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py (existing PolicyEngine.evaluate_gate path)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/chat_tools.py (chat_orchestrator submission path)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/self_healing.py
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py (autorun_ok)
- Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/003_gate_and_promotion_policy.sql (existing gate_evaluations schema)
- Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/189_oversight_instrumentation.sql (already applied)

Sections (no others):
1. THE HYBRID — one paragraph: WebAuthn Passkey + PWA + plan-hash envelope + extend gate_evaluations + kill _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES as authority (keep as UI hint).
2. TABLES — CREATE/ALTER for 185_mobile_capability_ledger.sql (capability_grants, device_enrollments, approval_requests), 186_gate_evaluations_grant_coverage.sql (ADD grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash), 187_webauthn_challenges.sql, 188_mobile_sessions.sql.
3. HTTP ENDPOINTS — POST /auth/webauthn/register/begin, /complete; /auth/webauthn/assert/begin, /complete; /auth/bootstrap/exchange; /approvals/request, /pending, /{id}/ratify, /{id}/revoke, /subscribe (SSE); /capability/grants/{id}/revoke; /mobile/metrics/snapshot.
4. PYTHON MODULES — runtime/capability/types.py, plan_envelope.py, resolver.py, approval_lifecycle.py, blast_radius.py, policy_drift.py, autonomy_chain.py; runtime/webauthn/ceremonies.py; surfaces/api/handlers/webauthn_auth.py, approvals.py, mobile_metrics.py.
5. TYPESCRIPT MODULES — surfaces/app/src/mobile/ (stub; actual build in Phase 5).
6. THE CALL GRAPH — numbered trace from chat_tools.py through plan_envelope.stamp → resolver.resolve_capability_grant → classify_control_intent → gate_evaluations insert with grant_ref.
7. WHERE _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES GOES — rename to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES in Phase 2; only UI consumers read it.
8. STOLEN-PHONE DEFENSE — step-up assertion <30s; CLI revoke-device; visible burn-rate ceiling.

Do not write code. Produce the doc; print the full path + section outline.

jobs.label: wave0_migration_capability_ledger
jobs.description: Write migrations 185, 186, 187 per the design doc.
jobs.outcome_goal: Three migration files apply cleanly. Tables capability_grants, device_enrollments, approval_requests, webauthn_challenges exist. gate_evaluations has grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash columns. All registered in workflow_migration_authority.json.
jobs.prompt:
Write the three migrations per docs/architecture/mobile_access_plan.md section 2.

1. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/185_mobile_capability_ledger.sql — capability_grants, device_enrollments, approval_requests with all columns, CHECK constraints, and indexes named.
2. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/186_gate_evaluations_grant_coverage.sql — ALTER TABLE gate_evaluations ADD COLUMN grant_ref TEXT NULL REFERENCES capability_grants(grant_id); ADD COLUMN plan_envelope_hash TEXT NULL; CREATE INDEX gate_evaluations_grant_ref_decided_at_idx ON gate_evaluations (grant_ref, decided_at DESC) WHERE grant_ref IS NOT NULL.
3. Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/187_webauthn_challenges.sql — webauthn_challenges with expiry index.

Constraints:
- Idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS).
- Every CHECK constraint named.
- ON DELETE RESTRICT on FKs — never cascade grant history.
- COMMENT ON TABLE on each.
- Register in Code&DBs/Workflow/system_authority/workflow_migration_authority.json and regenerate the Python generator artifact.

jobs.label: wave0_contract_types
jobs.description: Define frozen dataclasses mirroring table columns with from_row/to_row helpers.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime/capability/types.py defines CapabilityGrant, PlanEnvelope, DeviceEnrollment, ApprovalRequest; plan_hash is deterministic across retries.
jobs.prompt:
Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/ as a new package. Add:
- __init__.py exporting the dataclasses.
- types.py with @dataclass(frozen=True) definitions mirroring the table columns exactly:
  - CapabilityGrant(grant_id, issued_by, subject_run_id, subject_job_id, plan_hash, allowed_operations: tuple[str,...], write_scope: tuple[str,...], read_scope: tuple[str,...], budget_ceiling_cents, budget_consumed_cents, expires_at, issued_at, revoked_at, device_enrollment_id, signature).
  - PlanEnvelope(plan_hash, spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents, computed_at). Include classmethod from_intent(intent) with JSON-canonical sha256.
  - DeviceEnrollment(enrollment_id, public_key_cose: bytes, credential_id: bytes, aaguid, sign_count, created_at, revoked_at, label).
  - ApprovalRequest(request_id, run_id, job_label, intent_kind, intent_payload: Mapping, proposed_plan_envelope: PlanEnvelope, proposed_plan_hash, status: Literal['pending','ratified','revoked','expired'], created_at, decided_at, decided_by, resulting_grant_id).
- errors.py with CapabilityExhaustedError, GrantNotCoveringIntentError, GrantExpiredError, StepUpRequiredError.

No SQL. Pure types. Unit test at tests/unit/test_capability_types.py: plan_hash determinism, key-order independence, round-trip to_row/from_row.

jobs.label: wave1_webauthn_endpoints
jobs.description: Implement WebAuthn register/assert/step-up ceremonies via py_webauthn and wire into surfaces/api/.
jobs.outcome_goal: A browser can call /auth/webauthn/register/begin, complete registration with a software authenticator, then /auth/webauthn/assert/begin and /complete produce a session-bound assertion. Challenges persisted in webauthn_challenges; consumed on completion.
jobs.prompt:
Install py_webauthn (requirements.txt). Implement the ceremony as a standalone module.

Files:
- runtime/webauthn/__init__.py
- runtime/webauthn/ceremonies.py — generate_registration_options, verify_registration_response, generate_authentication_options, verify_authentication_response wrapping py_webauthn + persisting/consuming challenges via storage/postgres/webauthn_repository.py.
- storage/postgres/webauthn_repository.py — PostgresWebauthnRepository with insert_challenge, consume_challenge, insert_enrollment, get_enrollment_by_credential_id, bump_sign_count, revoke_enrollment.
- surfaces/api/handlers/webauthn_auth.py — aiohttp handlers for /auth/webauthn/register/begin, /complete, /auth/webauthn/assert/begin, /complete, /auth/bootstrap/exchange (single-use 5-min enrollment token).
- Wire into surfaces/api/rest.py at the existing /auth/ handler registration site.

Constraints:
- RP ID configurable via env MOBILE_RP_ID (default 'localhost' for dev).
- No bespoke signature scheme. Only py_webauthn's verified ceremony.
- Bootstrap token single-use, 5-min TTL, stored hashed (not plaintext).
- Step-up assertion = /auth/webauthn/assert/begin with purpose='step_up' and 30s expiry.
- Tests at tests/integration/test_webauthn_ceremony.py using py_webauthn's in-memory software authenticator.

jobs.label: wave1_session_middleware
jobs.description: Session middleware binding /mobile/* and /approvals/* to a verified WebAuthn assertion.
jobs.outcome_goal: Any /mobile/* or /approvals/* request without a valid non-expired session cookie bound to a device_enrollments row returns 401. Bootstrap path remains open.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/_mobile_session.py middleware that:
1. Reads httpOnly, SameSite=Strict session cookie 'praxis_mobile_session'.
2. Looks up session in mobile_sessions (migration 188_mobile_sessions.sql: session_id PK, enrollment_id FK, issued_at, expires_at, revoked_at). Sessions issued after successful verify_authentication_response.
3. Rejects revoked enrollment / expired session / missing cookie.
4. Attaches authed enrollment_id to request state.
5. For ratify/revoke: also require step-up assertion <30s in body.

Register on the mobile route group in rest.py. Allowlist /auth/bootstrap/exchange + /auth/webauthn/*.

Tests at tests/integration/test_mobile_session.py: missing cookie -> 401; revoked enrollment -> 401; valid session -> handler reached; ratify without step-up -> 403; ratify with stale (>30s) step-up -> 403.

jobs.label: wave2_plan_envelope_stamp
jobs.description: Stamp plan_hash on every submission intent at the chat_orchestrator entry point.
jobs.outcome_goal: chat_tools.request_workflow_submit_command computes a PlanEnvelope and attaches plan_hash to the ControlIntent BEFORE classify_control_intent runs. Hash is deterministic across retries.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/plan_envelope.py with:
- compute_plan_hash(spec_path, allowed_operations, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents) -> str (sha256 hex over JSON-canonical; sorted keys, normalized lists).
- stamp_plan_envelope(intent, *, spec_path, write_scope, read_scope, estimated_spend_cents) -> tuple[ControlIntent, PlanEnvelope] — returns intent with plan_hash populated plus the envelope.

Modify ControlIntent at runtime/control_commands.py to add optional plan_hash and plan_envelope fields (default None; resolver will require them).

Modify runtime/chat_tools.py at request_workflow_submit_command (around line 461-467) to call stamp_plan_envelope BEFORE classify_control_intent. write_scope / read_scope come from the workflow spec's per-job fields; estimated_spend_cents from lane_policy.estimate_spend.

Tests at tests/unit/test_plan_envelope.py: determinism, key-order independence, sensitivity to write_scope change, stamp produces populated plan_hash.

jobs.label: wave2_capability_resolver
jobs.description: Resolver answering 'is this intent covered by an active grant?'
jobs.outcome_goal: resolve_capability_grant(intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) returns CoverageDecision. Expired, revoked, over-budget, missing plan_hash match, or uncovered command all yield covered=False.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/resolver.py:

- CoverageDecision(covered: bool, grant_id: str | None, reason_code: str, remaining_budget_cents: int | None).
- resolve_capability_grant(conn, intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) -> CoverageDecision.
  SELECT * FROM capability_grants WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND expires_at > now() AND plan_hash = %s AND %s = ANY(allowed_operations) ORDER BY expires_at DESC LIMIT 1.
  No row -> (False, None, 'no_active_grant').
  budget_consumed + estimated > budget_ceiling -> (False, grant_id, 'budget_exhausted').
  intent.write_scope not subset of row.write_scope -> (False, grant_id, 'write_scope_outside_grant').
  Else (True, grant_id, 'covered', remaining_budget_cents).
- consume_grant_budget(conn, grant_id, spent_cents) — atomic UPDATE ... SET budget_consumed_cents = budget_consumed_cents + %s WHERE grant_id = %s AND budget_consumed_cents + %s <= budget_ceiling_cents; returns True if UPDATE matched else raises CapabilityExhaustedError.

Add storage/postgres/capability_repository.py with PostgresCapabilityRepository: insert_grant, get_active_grants_for_plan, revoke_grant, consume_budget.

Tests at tests/integration/test_capability_resolver.py using rollback-isolated conn: covered path, expired/revoked, plan_hash mismatch, budget exhausted, write_scope outside grant, concurrent consume race (second raises).

jobs.label: wave2_blast_radius_tracker
jobs.description: Runtime instrumentation for session_blast_radius + mid-plan gate (debate pillar 4).
jobs.outcome_goal: Every file mutation, external call, and spend event updates the active session_blast_radius row. When any dimension crosses its threshold in session_blast_radius_policy (seeded 'default': 50 files, 5 MiB, 20 external calls, $10), the runtime opens a mid-plan approval_request and writes gate_triggered_at + gate_triggered_dim — regardless of grant coverage.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189_oversight_instrumentation.sql is already applied. This job wires the runtime.

Files to create:
- runtime/capability/blast_radius.py:
    - BlastRadiusSnapshot dataclass mirroring the table.
    - open_session(conn, run_id) -> session_id: INSERT new active row.
    - record_mutation(conn, session_id, files_delta, bytes_delta): atomic UPDATE ... RETURNING *. After update, call check_thresholds.
    - record_external_call(conn, session_id, count_delta=1): bumps external_calls.
    - record_spend(conn, session_id, spend_cents_delta): bumps spend_cents.
    - check_thresholds(conn, row) -> ThresholdDecision: reads session_blast_radius_policy WHERE active=true.
    - trigger_gate(conn, session_id, triggered_dim, plan_envelope): sets gate_triggered_at/dim, calls approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request with intent_kind='blast_radius_threshold_crossed', returns approval_requests.request_id.
    - close_session(conn, session_id, reason).
- storage/postgres/blast_radius_repository.py — thin CRUD.

Call-site hooks (surgical):
1. runtime/control_commands.py main execute-intent path: after successful mutation, record_mutation(files_delta=1, bytes_delta=len(new_content)).
2. adapters/provider_transport.py every outbound HTTP call: record_external_call.
3. runtime/capability/resolver.py consume_grant_budget: after atomic spend consume, also record_spend.

Tests at tests/integration/test_blast_radius_tracker.py using rollback-isolated conn: counters start at 0; atomic increment under concurrency; threshold crossing fires trigger_gate; subsequent bumps don't re-fire (idempotent gate_triggered_at); gate_triggered_dim records specific dimension; close_session + reopen works.

jobs.label: wave2_policy_drift_detector
jobs.description: Emit policy_drift_events whenever runtime attempts a violation of an active operator_decisions policy (debate pillar 3).
jobs.outcome_goal: Every path neutralized during the anthropic CLI-only cutover now also emits a policy_drift_events row tagged policy_id='providers::anthropic', decision_ref='decision.2026-04-20.anthropic-cli-only-restored', detector_kind='anthropic_api_call_attempt' BEFORE returning its neutralized response.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189 already applied; policy_drift_events exists.

Files to create:
- runtime/capability/policy_drift.py:
    - emit_drift(conn, *, policy_id, decision_ref, detector_kind, violator_path, violator_run_id=None, action_summary, remediation_hint=None) -> drift_event_id. Never raises (log+swallow on insert failure).
    - recent_drift_count(conn, hours=24) -> int: SELECT count(*) WHERE resolved_at IS NULL AND detected_at > now() - make_interval(hours => %s).
    - drift_events(conn, hours=24, limit=50) -> list[DriftEvent]: newest-first.
    - resolve_drift(conn, drift_event_id, resolved_by).
- storage/postgres/policy_drift_repository.py — thin CRUD.

Instrument the five neutralized paths:
1. runtime/task_assembler._call_haiku — if any caller reaches the anthropic branch, emit_drift + raise.
2. adapters/provider_transport.py BUILTIN fallback — if anthropic client constructed with api_endpoint present, emit_drift.
3. adapters/docker_runner.py — if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY added back to _PROVIDER_AUTH_ENV_CANDIDATES, emit_drift at container spawn.
4. runtime/agent_spawner.py — same check on _PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS_FALLBACK.
5. registry/provider_onboarding/_probe.py anthropic_models_list — if operator pastes api_endpoint reaching HTTP, emit_drift + return [].

The emitter records; the neutralization still returns its safe value. Drift is observability, not enforcement.

Tests at tests/integration/test_policy_drift_detector.py: emit creates row; DB failure returns None (doesn't raise); recent_drift_count respects resolved_at; simulated misroute (monkeypatch) records one drift before raising.

jobs.label: wave2_autonomy_chain_ledger
jobs.description: Chain-length counter for auto-approved actions; human tap closes the chain (debate pillar 3 part 2).
jobs.outcome_goal: At most one active autonomy_chain_ledger row. classify_control_intent bumps depth_counter on AUTO_EXECUTE. approval_lifecycle.ratify closes the active chain and opens a new one, resetting depth to 0.
jobs.prompt:
Migration 189 applied; autonomy_chain_ledger exists with singleton partial-unique index.

Files to create:
- runtime/capability/autonomy_chain.py:
    - get_or_open_active(conn) -> chain_id: SELECT active row or INSERT. Singleton unique index serializes concurrent opens.
    - bump_depth(conn, run_id=None): SELECT chain_id via get_or_open_active, then UPDATE ... SET depth_counter = depth_counter + 1, last_updated = now(), current_run_id = COALESCE(%s, current_run_id) WHERE chain_id = %s AND terminated_at IS NULL.
    - close_on_human_tap(conn, reason='human_tap'): UPDATE SET terminated_at = now(), last_human_tap_at = now(), termination_reason = %s WHERE terminated_at IS NULL.
    - current_depth(conn) -> int: returns 0 if no active row.

Surgical call-site hooks (exactly two):
1. runtime/control_commands.classify_control_intent — when returning ControlPolicyDecision(mode=AUTO_EXECUTE, ...), call autonomy_chain.bump_depth(conn, run_id) before return.
2. runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.ratify_approval_request — at top of atomic block, call autonomy_chain.close_on_human_tap(conn).

Tests at tests/integration/test_autonomy_chain_ledger.py using rollback-isolated conn: empty table -> current_depth == 0; bump opens chain at depth 1; consecutive bumps increase monotonically; close terminates; next bump opens fresh chain at depth 1 (not continuing); partial unique index prevents two active rows under concurrency.
